title: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
subhead: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before the Vendée criminal court in western France starting March 25.

Emmanuel Grégoire's long run to Paris City Hall
subhead: The next mayor of Paris, who will be officially elected by the city council on Sunday, will now step into the spotlight after years in the shadow of his political mentors.

title: Danish PM Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats lead election but fall short of majority
subhead: With all votes counted, the left-wing bloc was credited with 84 seats in the 179-seat parliament and the right with 77. Frederikson, a favorite going into elections, has been praised for her leadership after fending off US President Trump's repeated demands to annex Greenland, a Danish autonomous territory.

title: 'I don't recognize any faces': Yohann's life with prosopagnosia
subhead: From childhood, Yohann Cordelle learned to navigate his prosopagnosia: a little-known neurological disorder that makes it impossible to remember faces. Now in his forties, he shares the coping strategies he has put in place.

title: Lionel Jospin, the man at the crossroads of France's 'irreconcilable lefts'
subhead: A moral compass for all the leading figures of the left and the architect of sweeping welfare reforms, the former prime minister was praised by the Socialists. Jean-Luc Mélenchon also admired him and could not stand his supporters criticizing Jospin.

title: France's traditional parties dream of a return to the classic left-right contest
subhead: The Socialists and right-wing Les Républicains can boast of successes in the second round of France's municipal elections on March 22. But both parties remain deeply weakened by the lack of a clear direction and the fragmentation of the political landscape, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

title: Lionel Jospin, French Socialist whose presidential ambitions were cut short
subhead: A major figure on the socialist left, Jospin held leading roles in French politics: prime minister, education minister, and head of the Socialist Party. He ran unsuccessfully for president in 1995 and 2002. He died at the age of 88.
